append_protocol.transaction: Inside one SQLite transaction, validate the expected prior projection, compute max committed run_sequence + 1, assign consecutive values to every record in the batch, validate the fully ordered batch, append it, and commit atomically. No sequence allocator exists outside that transaction.
append_protocol.idempotency: Every proposed record carries a deterministic operation_key. The store enforces uniqueness on (run_id, entity_type, operation_key). Replaying a committed operation returns the existing record and appends nothing.
append_protocol.operation_key_algorithm: sha256(RFC8785({contract_version, run_id, entity_type, operation_kind, causal_record_ids_sorted, policy_ordinal})). All inputs are persisted before planning. Provider-originated operations require a stable source_observation_key from the gateway; evidence without one opens recovery and cannot advance the effect.
append_protocol.recovery_planning: Recovery derives operation_key values only from persisted causal inputs using operation_key_algorithm, never from timestamps or newly generated UUIDs. UUIDv7 remains record identity, not replay identity.
append_protocol.sequence_failure: A rejected or rolled-back transaction consumes no run_sequence value; committed sequences remain gap-free.
append_protocol.batch_ordering: All records, regardless of entity type, are validated in ascending run_sequence. Evidence and acceptances required by a StateEvent must have lower run_sequence values, including when committed in the same batch.
store_constraints: unique(run_id, run_sequence) across the shared non-recovery stream
append_protocol.controller_fencing: The store maintains one active controller_generation under an OS-backed exclusive writer lease. Every append transaction includes and verifies that generation. Startup obtains a newer generation atomically; stale generations cannot append.

WorkflowRun.description: Orchestrates multi-effect workflows by coordinating child Runs with causal dependencies. Each child Run owns exactly one effect_key.

fields.children: array of {run_id: UUIDv7, effect_key: string, depends_on: [UUIDv7 run_ids], dependency_failure_policy: cancel_dependents | block_and_escalate}

Run.description: A single-effect execution unit. Owned by at most one WorkflowRun. Holds exactly one effect_key.

fields.declared_progress_events: string[] (what counts as liveness for no-progress timeout)
fields.output_contract_ref: ArtifactRef (nullable; content-addressed JSON contract declaring required outputs)
fields.run_sequence: integer (global monotonic per-run, shared across all run-scoped entities - transactional, gap-free)

fields.attempt_number: integer (separate business ordering per-run, not the shared run_sequence)
fields.effect_key: string (same as Run.effect_key, stable across attempts)

fields.run_id: UUIDv7 (references Run; Run and its EffectIntent are created in one atomic batch)

ProviderObservation.description: Raw, unverified provider attestation. Factual record of what the provider reported. Never conflated with controller verdict.

fields.source_observation_key: string (stable gateway/provider event identity; required before this observation can advance an effect)

EffectAcceptance.description: Controller verdict on a provider observation. Separated from raw observation. A pending record may be followed by exactly one final accepted or rejected record; final verdicts are irreversible.

fields.verification_details: json (optional - artifact receipt, process termination proof, etc.)

FailureEvidence.description: Controller-verified evidence that permits a failed terminal transition. Retry or recovery exhaustion is not failure evidence.

fields.source_record_ids: UUIDv7[] (non-empty references to persisted observations, supervisor receipts, or artifact receipts)
fields.verification_details: json (deterministic controller checks and authority attestations)

StateEvent.description: Records Run lifecycle phase transitions. Recovery is orthogonal; the Run stays in its last non-terminal phase while recovery is open.

fields.event_type: requested | dispatching | active | provider_completed | output_draining | cancellation_requested | terminal
fields.terminal_reason: no_progress_timeout | user_requested | orchestrator_requested | dependency_failed | dispatch_rejected | provider_reported_failure | output_persistence_failed | null (if non-terminal)

ProcessSupervisorReceipt.description: Platform-agnostic process identity and ownership record created before the process resumes execution. Termination verification uses the ownership_handle attestation, not PID alone.

fields.controller_nonce: string (issued before process start, prevents PID reuse attacks)
fields.os_start_fingerprint: string (platform-specific process identity - e.g., creation timestamp + parent PID + image hash)
fields.ownership_handle: string (platform-specific retained handle - Windows job object, Linux cgroup path, Docker container ID, k8s pod UID)
fields.supervisor_attestation: json (optional - external system attestation of process identity)

ArtifactRef.description: Content-addressed artifact identity. The digest is the immutable root of trust; location/resolver metadata is auxiliary.

ArtifactReceipt.description: Immutable proof of stored output. References ArtifactRef (content digest), not a mutable URI.

fields.resolver_metadata: json (optional - URI, bucket, registry path - auxiliary, never authoritative)

CancellationIntent.description: Declares why a run is being cancelled. no_progress_timeout is a reason here, never a direct terminal state.

CancellationStageEvent.description: Tracks granular progress through cancellation. Projection derives overall cancellation status. Recovery can continue from any stage.

RecoveryCycle.description: Orthogonal recovery stream. Opened when the controller detects a need to reconcile after crash, gap, or missing observation. Run remains in its last phase; effects pause except cancellation.

fields.resumed_phase: requested | dispatching | active | provider_completed | output_draining | cancellation_requested (the Run's last known non-terminal phase when recovery was triggered)

RecoveryProbe.description: A single probe of an external authority during a recovery cycle. Probe results are observations, not assumed deterministic.

fields.probe_result: json (attestation or status from external authority)

RecoveryOutcome.description: The result of a recovery cycle. Determines whether the run can resume or escalation is needed.

RecoveryEscalation.description: Appended when recovery is exhausted or an escalation condition is met. Has a declared runtime route; routing failure creates a system escalation and visible blocked state.

fields.declared_route: string (reference to investigator/orchestrator - agent ID, queue name, webhook)
fields.escalation_payload: json (typed escalation data including effect_key, attempt_count, last_observation, probe_results)

RetryPolicy.description: Append-only retry configuration per effect_key. Every attempt and outcome is appended.

fields.observation_wait_timeout_ms: integer (nullable - max wait for provider response before triggering recovery probe)
fields.escalation_timeout_ms: integer (default: 300000 - if escalation not acknowledged, produces unattended_escalation)

run_state_machine.description: Run lifecycle. Recovery is orthogonal (RecoveryCycle stream). no_progress_timeout is a CancellationIntent.reason, never a direct state. Terminal cancelled with reason=no_progress_timeout only after graceful abort, 10s grace, and owned-tree verification.

invalid_transitions.description: No transition out of succeeded, failed, cancelled.
Cannot jump from requested directly to active (must pass through dispatching).
provider_completed cannot go directly to succeeded (must drain output first).
Cannot enter output_draining from any state except provider_completed.

Once a cancellation_requested StateEvent exists (by run_sequence), no subsequent StateEvent can transition to succeeded or to any non-terminal state other than cancellation_requested itself. Late provider completion may preserve artifacts but cannot produce succeeded.
Silence alone is not failure; requires CancellationIntent with no_progress_timeout reason followed by full graceful abort, NOT a direct failed transition.

When multiple StateEvents for the same run are appended in a single batch, the event with the lowest run_sequence is applied first. If the resulting state makes the higher-sequence event an invalid transition, that event is rejected per INV-005.

required: at least one FailureEvidence with lower run_sequence and category matching terminal_reason
inconclusive probes, recovery exhaustion, or escalation routing failure are never sufficient for failed
if a process was launched: cancellationstageevent_ownership_verified, supervisor termination attestation, applicable 10-second grace, and owned-tree verification
if no process was launched: durable supervisor attestation that no owned process exists
cancelled.terminal_reason_propagation: terminal_reason mirrors the run's single CancellationIntent.reason (no_progress_timeout, user_requested, orchestrator_requested, recovery_escalation, dependency_failed)

ordering_rules: EffectIntent MUST be committed before external effect execution begins.
The controller appends ProviderObservation after provider response (or timeout) - factual record only.
The controller appends EffectAcceptance (pending) in the same atomic batch where possible; after a crash, recovery fills the gap before the effect may advance.
After artifact verification or process confirmation, a new EffectAcceptance (accepted|rejected) is appended that supersedes the pending one.
An observation has at most one final EffectAcceptance; accepted and rejected verdicts can never be superseded.
Crash or missing observation after execution -> recovery probe, never assumed failure.
Stable effect_key reused across attempts; each attempt has distinct attempt_id.

at_most_once.claim: Exactly-once external effects are NOT claimed.
at_most_once.guarantee: At most one accepted EffectAcceptance per effect_key across all attempts, enforced by a partial unique store index. If an effect_key already has an accepted acceptance, all subsequent EffectAcceptances for any attempt of that effect_key MUST have controller_verdict=rejected.
at_most_once.idempotent_reconciliation: Duplicate provider successes for an already-accepted effect_key are recorded as ProviderObservation(fact) with EffectAcceptance(rejected, reason='already_succeeded').

fields.observation_wait_timeout_ms: integer (nullable - configurable; if set, missing provider response within window triggers recovery probe, not failure)
fields.escalation_timeout_ms: integer (default: 300000 - if typed escalation not acknowledged within window, run transitions to system_escalation, never falsely failed)

behavior.exhaustion_action: typed_escalation | investigation_event (never a failed terminal; escalation has declared route; routing failure -> system_escalation -> visible blocked state)

stages: requested (CancellationStageEvent appended; CancellationIntent with reason appended)
termination_requested (CancellationStageEvent appended; abort signal sent to process supervisor)
process_terminated (CancellationStageEvent appended; ProcessSupervisorReceipt termination verified via ownership_handle)
ownership_verified (CancellationStageEvent appended; owned tree confirmed stopped)
cancellation_model.projection_derived_progress: Cancellation progress is derived from CancellationStageEvent stream, not from Run state alone. Recovery can continue cancellation from any stage.
cancellation_model.artifact_preservation: Partial artifacts and session state preserved on cancellation; ArtifactReceipts already appended remain intact.
cancellation_model.grace_period: 10 seconds after an abort signal before forced termination; not applicable when the supervisor attests that no process was launched.
cancellation_model.pre_launch_cancellation: If cancellation wins before process launch, the supervisor appends an attestation that no owned process exists; termination_requested and process_terminated stages are not fabricated, and ownership_verified records the no-process proof.
cancellation_model.no_progress_timeout_flow: 10-minute silence (no declared progress events within window) -> CancellationIntent appended with reason=no_progress_timeout -> cancellation_requested StateEvent appended -> full graceful cancellation flow -> terminal cancelled with terminal_reason=no_progress_timeout only after owned-tree verification.
cancellation_model.intent_cardinality: Exactly one CancellationIntent per run, enforced by a unique store constraint. Later cancellation requests are recorded as audit inputs but cannot replace the original reason or create another intent.

recovery_model.description: Orthogonal to Run lifecycle. Recovery is triggered by: controller crash/restart, sequence gap detection, missing observation after wait_timeout, or inconclusive external authority state.
startup_procedure: Deterministically reduce all streams from a consistent event snapshot (no external input needed for the reduction itself).
Find all non-terminal runs and open recovery cycles for any with missing observations, gaps, or ambiguous state.
For each recovery cycle: probe external authorities (harness session, OS process table via supervisor handle, git/PR status, artifact store).
Probe results are appended as RecoveryProbe records (facts, not assumptions).
Append RecoveryOutcome: resolved (run can continue), escalated (exhaustion -> RecoveryEscalation), blocked (run stays in phase, effects paused), or inconclusive (retry).
Never rewrite or mutate existing history.
If a cancellation_requested StateEvent exists for the run, recovery MUST continue the cancellation flow; recovery MUST NOT transition the run to active.

determinism.guarantee: Recovery reduction over a consistent event snapshot (no concurrent appends, no live probes) is deterministic and idempotent.
determinism.live_probes: Probing external authorities during recovery produces new observations (RecoveryProbe records); these are NOT assumed deterministic and may differ across recovery passes. The recovery algorithm tolerates non-deterministic probe results gracefully.

exhaustion.action: RecoveryEscalation appended with escalation_type=recovery_exhausted and declared_route to investigator/orchestrator.
exhaustion.escalation_timeout: If escalation not acknowledged within escalation_timeout_ms, a system_escalation RecoveryEscalation is appended and the run enters a visible blocked/reconciling state, never falsely failed.

guarantees: Idempotent recovery planning over a consistent snapshot produces the same emission keys; transactional uniqueness makes a repeated application append nothing.
No human required for operational recovery.
All reductions are deterministic from persisted streams.
Inconclusive probes never cause a failed terminal state.
recovery_model.process_supervision_requirement: Each platform adapter must use either a supervisor that survives controller restart and retains the ownership handle, or an OS primitive that atomically terminates the owned tree when the controller handle closes. If neither behavior can be attested and recovered, dispatch is rejected before launch.

projections.rebuild_contract: Projections are rebuilt from a consistent SQLite snapshot to avoid races with concurrent appends. Rebuilt projections from the same snapshot must match projections originally computed from that same snapshot. Only the active fenced controller generation may publish projections.
defined: active_runs: all runs not in terminal state (derived from latest StateEvent per run by run_sequence).
pending_intents: EffectIntents where no accepted EffectAcceptance exists for their effect_key.
recovery_open: runs with a RecoveryCycle whose RecoveryOutcome has not yet reached resolved, escalated, or exhausted cycles.
retry_exhausted: runs where attempt_count >= max_attempts with no accepted EffectAcceptance per effect_key.
process_ownership_active: latest ProcessSupervisorReceipt per run where process not verified terminated.
cancellation_progress: derived from CancellationStageEvent stream - current stage per cancellation_id, ordered by run_sequence.

escalation_blocked: runs with RecoveryEscalation where routing_status=failed or escalation_type=system_escalation and no acknowledgment received; these runs are visibly blocked, never falsely resolved.
workflow_graph: derived from WorkflowRun.children - causal dependency tree and per-child run status.

invariants.statement: No field in any entity is ever mutated after initial append.

run_sequence per Run is strictly monotonic and gap-free across all non-recovery run-scoped entities: Run, EffectIntent, RunAttempt, ProviderObservation, EffectAcceptance, FailureEvidence, StateEvent, ProcessSupervisorReceipt, ArtifactReceipt, CancellationIntent, CancellationStageEvent, and RetryPolicy. Values are allocated only inside the atomic append transaction.
Timestamps are never used for ordering; only run_sequence fields determine event order.
Every EffectIntent is committed before any corresponding ProviderObservation exists (by run_sequence).
No Run with terminal StateEvent (terminal_subtype in [succeeded, failed, cancelled]) can have future StateEvents appended.
ProcessSupervisorReceipt.controller_nonce is unique per controller instance and verified before any process signal. Nonce + ownership_handle together form the identity root.
PID reuse cannot cause incorrect process termination. Termination verification uses ownership_handle attestation (supervisor-witnessed), not PID alone.
Same effect_key can have multiple RunAttempts; each attempt_id is globally unique (UUIDv7).

At most one accepted EffectAcceptance (controller_verdict=accepted) per effect_key across all attempts, enforced by a partial unique store index. If an accepted acceptance exists for effect_key K, all subsequent EffectAcceptances for any attempt of K must have controller_verdict=rejected.

Terminal succeeded requires at least one ArtifactReceipt with a valid ArtifactRef (sha256 digest, algorithm, byte_size) if the Run declared output expectations. The digest is the immutable identity; location metadata is auxiliary.

Cancellation preserves partial artifacts. Terminal cancelled requires CancellationStageEvent.ownership_verified and either: for a launched process, ProcessSupervisorReceipt termination verification via ownership_handle; or before launch, a durable supervisor attestation that no owned process exists.

Silence (no declared progress events) cannot transition directly to failed. Timeout triggers CancellationIntent with reason=no_progress_timeout, which flows through the full cancellation lifecycle to terminal cancelled.

Once a cancellation_requested StateEvent exists for a run (by run_sequence), no subsequent StateEvent can have event_type=active, provider_completed, output_draining, or terminal_subtype=succeeded. Cancellation is irreversible.
recovery_sequence per run is strictly monotonic and independent of run_sequence. Recovery cycles, probes, outcomes, and escalations are orthogonal to the Run lifecycle.
RecoveryProbes for a RecoveryCycle are always appended (by recovery_sequence) before the RecoveryOutcome for that same cycle.

Failed terminal StateEvent requires a prior FailureEvidence whose category matches terminal_reason and no unresolved external effect. Retry exhaustion, inconclusive probes, recovery exhaustion, and escalation routing failure are never sufficient to transition to failed.

CancellationStageEvent stages follow a logically ordered sequence per cancellation_id: requested -> provider_acknowledged -> termination_requested -> process_terminated -> ownership_verified. Stages may be skipped (rapid resolution) but out-of-order stages are forbidden.
All entity identity fields use UUIDv7 format. Collisions are treated as invariant violations and rejected at append time.

Every RecoveryEscalation has a declared_route (reference to investigator/orchestrator). If routing_status=failed or the escalation is not acknowledged within escalation_timeout_ms, a system_escalation RecoveryEscalation is appended and the run enters a visible blocked/reconciling state. The run is never falsely transitioned to failed.

A dependent child cannot dispatch before all dependencies succeed. If any dependency fails or is cancelled, the runtime deterministically applies that child's immutable dependency_failure_policy: cancel_dependents appends CancellationIntent(reason=dependency_failed), while block_and_escalate leaves it requested and appends a routed RecoveryEscalation.

When multiple events within the same run_scope are appended in a single batch, the event with the lowest run_sequence is applied first. If the resulting state makes a higher-sequence event invalid, that event is rejected (per INV-005 for terminal and INV-013 for cancellation one-way).

ProviderObservation and EffectAcceptance are separate entities. A ProviderObservation can exist temporarily without an EffectAcceptance; recovery must append pending before the effect can be considered complete. Verification may append exactly one final accepted or rejected verdict for that observation, and a final verdict can never be superseded.

Every non-recovery run-scoped entity carries run_id and shares the transactional, gap-free run_sequence namespace listed by INV-002. Recovery entities use the independent recovery_sequence from INV-014. Run, EffectIntent, RetryPolicy, and the initial requested StateEvent are created in one atomic batch.

Only the controller appends to the source of truth. Providers, harnesses, supervisors, git systems, and artifact stores supply evidence; the controller records that evidence without granting them write authority.

Every appended record has operation_key = sha256(RFC8785({contract_version, run_id, entity_type, operation_kind, causal_record_ids_sorted, policy_ordinal})), unique within (run_id, entity_type). All inputs are persisted before planning. Replaying returns the existing record and consumes no run_sequence. UUIDv7 identities are never replay keys.

A process cannot resume until the trusted process supervisor has durably established either a recoverable ownership handle or crash-close termination semantics and the controller has committed its receipt. Unsupported adapters fail before launch.
Exactly one CancellationIntent exists per run, enforced by a unique store constraint. Its reason is immutable and is the only source for terminal cancelled reason propagation.
Every append is fenced by the active controller_generation under an OS-backed exclusive writer lease. A stale or concurrent controller generation cannot append records or publish projections.

All records in a batch are validated in ascending run_sequence across entity types. Any evidence, final acceptance, or artifact required for a terminal StateEvent must have a lower run_sequence than that StateEvent.

acceptance_tests.unit: falsifiable crash and concurrency scenarios (run < 100ms each)

scenarios.description: Two identical EffectIntents with same effect_key and same run_sequence race; only first appended, second rejected as duplicate.
ProviderObservation appended referencing unknown intent_id -> invariant violation (INV-004), rejected.

Run active, provider reports completion externally, controller crashes. Recovery finds provider completion via a harness probe, appends ProviderObservation(completed) and EffectAcceptance(pending) before StateEvent(provider_completed), using stable operation keys. Never marks as failed.
StateEvent with timestamp earlier than predecessor but higher run_sequence -> ordering by run_sequence, timestamp ignored. Projection correct.

All attempts appended, max_attempts reached, no accepted EffectAcceptance. Typed RecoveryEscalation appended with escalation_type=recovery_exhausted and declared_route. If routing fails, system_escalation appended. Run blocked, never quietly failed.

Batch append: provider_completed at run_sequence=N, cancellation_requested at run_sequence=N+1. Lower sequence applied first: Run enters provider_completed. Higher sequence applied: cancellation_requested valid from provider_completed. Run enters cancellation_requested, terminal cancelled expected. Artifacts preserved but no succeeded.

Run in cancellation_requested (terminal pending). Provider later sends completion. ProviderObservation appended (factual). EffectAcceptance appended with controller_verdict=rejected (cancellation is one-way per INV-013). ArtifactReceipt may be appended to preserve partial output. Run remains in cancellation_requested, cannot transition to succeeded.

Process A owned by Run X terminates; controller_nonce N1. New Process B reuses same PID. Controller checks ProcessSupervisorReceipt: ownership_handle mismatch (different process). Termination of B refused. RecoveryProbe appended documenting stale PID.

Effect EK-1 has attempts A1, A2. A1 gets accepted EffectAcceptance. A2 also receives provider success. ProviderObservation appended for A2 (fact). EffectAcceptance for A2 must be rejected per INV-009 (already succeeded). Only one accepted per effect_key.

Two transactions with distinct operation_keys concurrently attempt accepted EffectAcceptance records for the same effect_key. The partial unique index commits exactly one; the loser records a rejected already_succeeded verdict on retry.

Run in output_draining. Controller restarts. Recovery finds provider_completed StateEvent, opens RecoveryCycle with resumed_phase=output_draining, probes artifact store (not found). Appends RecoveryProbe and RecoveryOutcome=inconclusive. Run stays in output_draining. Recovery retries with backoff. No duplicate StateEvent appended.
Two concurrent attempts to append terminal StateEvent (terminal_subtype=succeeded) for same run. Only first succeeds; second rejected as invalid transition from terminal per INV-005.
Entity stream receives event with run_sequence N+2 while N+1 missing -> gap detected. RecoveryCycle opened. Recovery probe investigates gap source. Never processes out of order.

Run active with no declared progress events for 9 minutes. No action. At 10 minutes, CancellationIntent appended with reason=no_progress_timeout. cancellation_requested StateEvent appended. Graceful abort with 10s grace. CancellationStageEvents appended. Terminal cancelled with terminal_reason=no_progress_timeout only after owned-tree verification.

Cancellation completes. ProcessSupervisorReceipt with ownership_handle verified by supervisor attestation. Process confirmed terminated via handle, not PID alone. CancellationStageEvent.ownership_verified appended. Run transitions to terminal cancelled.

Recovery planner applied twice to the same consistent SQLite snapshot produces the same deterministic emission keys. Applying the plan twice appends each keyed event once; the second transaction appends nothing. Live probe results are already persisted inputs to this test.

Output drain complete, ArtifactReceipt appended with ArtifactRef (sha256 digest, algorithm, byte_size). Run transitions to succeeded. Without valid ArtifactRef, transition rejected - run stays in output_draining.
Run enters provider_completed. output_draining required before terminal succeeded. Direct provider_completed->succeeded is invalid. Artifact drain must complete.
Run cancelled mid-output. Partial ArtifactReceipts with valid ArtifactRef already appended remain intact. Cancellation proceeds. Artifacts not lost.

Retry budget exhausted for effect EK-1. RecoveryEscalation appended with escalation_type, effect_key, attempt_count, last_observation, and declared_route="queue:investigator-east". If delivered, escalation acknowledged. If routing failed, system_escalation appended. Run blocked, never quietly failed.
Capture consistent SQLite snapshot. Compute projections from snapshot. Drop projections. Rebuild from same snapshot. Rebuilt projections match originals exactly.
Attempt to update run_sequence field on existing StateEvent -> rejected. Only append allowed per INV-001.

Harness session attempts to append StateEvent or ProviderObservation directly: rejected. It can return provider and session evidence to the controller, which is the only writer to the source of truth.

ProviderObservation appended with provider_assertion=completed. EffectAcceptance appended separately with controller_verdict=pending. After artifact verification, new EffectAcceptance appended with controller_verdict=accepted. Observation is fact; acceptance is verdict.

Output drain attempted. Provider reports completion (ProviderObservation appended). Artifact store unreachable. EffectAcceptance stays pending. Run stays in output_draining. Recovery probe retries artifact check. No false success per INV-010.

Run in output_draining crashes. RecoveryCycle appended with resumed_phase=output_draining. Recovery knows exact prior phase without full history scan. Probes artifact store. Outcome determined based on prior phase context.

cancellation_requested appended for run R. Recovery triggered. Recovery sees cancellation_requested StateEvent. Per INV-013 and recovery model, recovery MUST NOT transition to active. Recovery continues cancellation flow: probes harness, appends CancellationStageEvent, eventually reaches terminal cancelled.

Run in active. Controller crashes. Recovery probes provider - TCP timeout (inconclusive). RecoveryOutcome=inconclusive appended. Recovery retries with backoff. Run NEVER transitions to failed from inconclusive probe alone per INV-016.

WorkflowRun with children: child A (depends_on=[]), child B (depends_on=[A]). Child A can dispatch immediately. Child B attempts dispatch before A reaches succeeded -> rejected per INV-020. After A succeeds, B can dispatch.

A dependency reaches failed or cancelled. A child with cancel_dependents receives exactly one CancellationIntent(reason=dependency_failed). A child with block_and_escalate remains requested and gets a routed escalation. Neither child remains silently stuck.

RecoveryEscalation appended with declared_route='queue:nonexistent'. Routing fails (routing_status=failed). escalation_timeout_ms elapses. System_escalation RecoveryEscalation appended. Run enters visible blocked/reconciling state - never falsely transitioned to succeeded or failed per INV-019.

CancellationIntent appended. Provider responds with acknowledged_cancellation. ProviderObservation appended (provider_assertion=acknowledged_cancellation). CancellationStageEvent appended with stage=provider_acknowledged. Recovery continues from this stage if crash occurs.

Effect executed, observation_wait_timeout_ms=30000. 30s pass without provider response. Timeout triggers RecoveryCycle open (not failed). Recovery probes provider. Provider found alive, effect still running. RecoveryOutcome=resolved. Run continues in active. Never transitioned to failed.

ProcessSupervisorReceipt created with controller_nonce N1 and ownership_handle H1 at startup. On termination check, os_pid matches but ownership_handle H2 (different process). Termination refused per INV-006. RecoveryProbe appended documenting PID collision.

Controller crashes after committing a recovery append but before acknowledging it. Restart derives the same operation_key from persisted causal inputs. The store returns the existing record, appends nothing, and consumes no run_sequence.

Controller crashes while a process is active. The platform supervisor either retains the ownership handle across restart or atomically terminates the tree on handle close. Recovery obtains a supervisor attestation; it never relies on PID alone.
Platform adapter cannot retain an ownership handle and cannot prove crash-close termination. Dispatch is rejected before child process resume; no active StateEvent is appended.

Cancellation wins while the run is requested or dispatching before process launch. Supervisor attests that no owned process exists. ownership_verified records that proof and the run reaches cancelled without fabricated termination events or a grace delay.
An observation already has final verdict accepted. A later rejected verdict for the same observation_id is rejected. The same applies in reverse; at most one final verdict exists per observation.

Batch contains: StateEvent(terminal_subtype=succeeded, run_sequence=5) and StateEvent(terminal_subtype=failed, run_sequence=6). Sequence 5 applied first: run enters succeeded (terminal). Sequence 6: rejected per INV-005 (already terminal). Per INV-021.

A batch proposes StateEvent(succeeded) before its required accepted EffectAcceptance or ArtifactReceipt by run_sequence. The whole transaction is rejected. Reordering evidence before terminal state commits atomically.

A run already has CancellationIntent(reason=no_progress_timeout). A later orchestrator cancellation request cannot create a second intent or replace terminal reason; it is retained only as audit input.

Controller generation G1 loses its writer lease and G2 starts. G1 attempts an append after G2 is active. The store rejects G1 before sequence allocation; projections and history contain only G2 writes.

Every attempt is reconciled and max_attempts is reached without accepted success or typed FailureEvidence. Runtime appends routed escalation and remains visibly blocked; a failed StateEvent is rejected.

Gateway returns provider evidence without a stable source_observation_key. The controller records a recovery condition but cannot append a ProviderObservation that advances the effect; no duplicate or terminal state is inferred.

Run in active with intermittent provider. Recovery cycles execute: 1 (inconclusive), 2 (inconclusive), ... up to max_recovery_cycles=5. Cycle 5: RecoveryOutcome=escalated. RecoveryEscalation appended with escalation_type=recovery_exhausted. Run blocked, not transitioned to failed without verified provider failure per INV-016.

residual_limits: no_assumed_failure: Missing observation without explicit provider failure is not failure; it triggers recovery, not a terminal state.
no_mutable_status: No entity contains a mutable status field; state is derived from StateEvent and Projection streams.

no_human_operational_recovery: Recovery is algorithmic. Failed escalation routing leaves a visible blocked state and follows configured system routes; it does not silently convert an operational problem into required human work.
no_pid_reliance: OS PID is observed, not authoritative. ProcessSupervisorReceipt.ownership_handle is the identity root for termination verification.
no_exactly_once_claim: External effects are at-most-one-accepted per effect_key with idempotent reconciliation, not exactly-once.

no_silence_as_failure: Absence of declared progress events within configured window triggers CancellationIntent with no_progress_timeout reason, flowing through full cancellation lifecycle to terminal cancelled - never direct failed.

no_terminal_without_evidence: Terminal states require prior verified evidence: accepted effect and required artifact receipt (succeeded), typed FailureEvidence (failed), or supervisor proof that the owned process tree terminated or never launched (cancelled). Retry or recovery exhaustion remains blocked, never failed.
no_provider_completed_to_terminal: provider_completed is non-terminal until output/artifact persistence completes via output_draining.
no_cancellation_reversal: Once cancellation_requested is appended, the run can never return to active or transition to succeeded. Cancellation is a one-way door.
no_conflated_observation_and_verdict: ProviderObservation records raw provider facts; EffectAcceptance records controller verdict after verification. They are distinct entities.
no_mutable_artifact_identity: Artifact identity is content-addressed (ArtifactRef with sha256 digest). URI/location is auxiliary metadata, never the root of trust.
no_inconclusive_probes_as_failure: Recovery probes that return inconclusive results must not transition a run to failed without explicit verified provider failure or retry exhaustion.
